title: Rajasthan sizzles as temperature rises amid heatwave-like conditions - ThePrint
full_text:
Jaipur, May 18 (PTI) Rajasthan continued to reel under intense heat, with Pilani and Chittorgarh’s maximum temperature touching 46.2 degrees Celsius each, the Meteorological Department said on Monday.
Sri Ganganagar recorded 46.1 degrees Celsius, Bikaner 46 degrees, Vanasthali 45.6 degrees, Fatehpur 45.5 degrees, Kota 45.3 degrees, while Barmer and Churu each recorded 45.1 degrees.
Almost all cities across recorded maximum temperatures above 41 degrees, while the state capital, Jaipur, recorded 43.6 degrees.
According to the Jaipur Meteorological Centre, the weather is likely to remain dry across the state for the rest of the week.
Heatwave conditions are expected at isolated places in the border areas of western Rajasthan and parts of the south-eastern region, it said.
The department also forecast strong dusty surface winds blowing at speeds of 20-30 kmph over western, northern and eastern parts of the state in the next four to five days. Authorities have advised people to take precautions against heat exposure as temperatures are likely to remain high in the coming days. PTI AG KSI KSI
